div1.entryFree:
Link, vb. (cf. Enlink). 1) tr. to chain, to tie, to join: coupled and --ed together, John III, 1, 228. --ed in friendship, H6C IV, 1, 116. With to: to l. my friend to a stale, Ado IV, 1, 66. Hml. I, 5, 55. Ant. I, 2, 193. With with: --ed in love with a lady, H6A V, 5, 76.
2) intr. to form a connexion, to be connected: I were loath to l. with him that were not lawful chosen, H6C III, 3, 115.
